data.title: November asking rents drop 0.7% Y/Y for apartments to lowest level in over two years: Redfin
data.description:
The median U.S. asking rent for apartments fell 0.7% Y/Y in November to $1,595, touching the lowest level since March 2022, according to a Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) report released on Wednesday. The median rent is now 6.2% lower than the all-time high of $1,700 in August 2022.

The data marked the 19th straight month of declining median rent price per square foot, dropping 2.2% Y/Y to $1.79, the first time it's lower than $1.80 since November 2021.

An increased supply of apartments led to declining rents and higher vacancy rates. Nationally, apartment completions increased 22.6% Y/Y to the highest level in over 12 years in Q2.The vacancy rate for buildings with five or more units rose to 8% in Q3, the highest level since early 2021, Redfin said.

"Renters in areas where construction has boomed are in a sweet spot right now. Affordability is improving as rents fall and wages rise, and there is increased choice with more and more new apartment buildings opening," said Redfin Senior Economist Sheharyar Bokhari.

But as construction starts slow, rents will eventually edge back up. Meanwhile, 2025 appears to remain a renter's market.

Median asking rents slid the most in Austin, Texas, -12.4% to $1,445, followed by Tampa, Florida, -11.3% to $1,740. Cleveland saw the biggest increase, up 10.6%, followed by Louisville, Kentucky, up 10.2%.

Apartment REIT stocks, however, mostly traded in the green on Wednesday. Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) stock edged down 0.1%, Independence Realty Trust (NYSE:IRT) +1.3%, AvalongBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) +0.5%, Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) +0.1%, and Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) +0.2%.

data.title: Mondelēz International approves new $9B share repurchase authorization
data.description:
• The Board of Directors of Mondelēz International (NASDAQ:MDLZ) has approved a new share repurchase authorization of up to $9B of Class A common stock, effective Jan. 1, 2025.
  • The new authorization, effective until Dec. 31, 2027, will replace the current $6B authorization, of which ~$2.8B is presently remaining and would otherwise expire on Dec. 31, 2025.
  • The company may repurchase the shares in open market transactions, privately negotiated transactions, or a combination of the foregoing.
  • The Co. has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.47/share of Class A common stock payable on Jan. 14, 2025, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on Dec. 31, 2024.

data.title: Bank of Canada cuts interest rate by another 50 basis points as expected
data.description:
The Bank of Canada cut its key interest rate by 50bps for a second consecutive time in its December decision, as expected by markets. This is the fifth consecutive decrease since June and brings the rate to 3.25%.

"With inflation around 2%, the economy in excess supply, and recent indicators tilted towards softer growth than projected, Governing Council decided to reduce the policy rate by a further 50 basis points to support growth and keep inflation close to the middle of the 1-3% target range." according to statement by Bank of Canada.

Macklem statement says "with the policy rate now substantially lower, we anticipate a more gradual approach to monetary policy if the economy evolves broadly as expected." governor Tiff Macklem said.

"Canada’s economy grew by 1% in the third quarter, which was slower than we expected. Recent data also suggest growth will be lower than projected in the final quarter of this year." Macklem noted.

ETFs: (EWC), (FLCA).

data.title: Raymond James upgrades EastGroup Properties, downgrades Prologis
data.description:
Raymond James upgraded the industrial REIT EastGroup Properties (NYSE:EGP) on the back of strong fundamentals as well as an attractive valuation, but lowered its recommendation on Prologis (NYSE:PLD) over a less-compelling risk/reward.

The investment bank upgraded EGP to Strong Buy from Outperform, and increased the price target to $200 from $185.

"Our EGP upgrade is driven by 1) our belief that NOI growth will see a slower rate of deceleration vs. peers due to EGP's smaller, multitenant industrial buildings ("shallow bay") that face relatively less supply pressure and historically offer more resilient demand than larger distribution facilities; 2) its attractive valuation (both absolute and relative) coupled with sector-leading AFFO growth through 2026 (2024-2026E CAGR of 13% vs. peer average 9%); and 3) strong balance sheet and capacity for external growth activity; and 4) smaller size that makes any external growth more impactful," said analysts led by Jonathan Hughes.

Meanwhile, PLD was downgraded to Outperform from Strong Buy, with the price target reduced to $125 from $130.

"We believe PLD's risk/reward is less-compelling and no longer warrants our highest conviction rating. That said, we remain constructive on PLD due to 1) its attractive valuation (both absolute and relative) coupled with solid AFFO growth through 2026 (2024-2026E CAGR of 9%); and 2) long-term upside potential — both financial and sentiment — from the unique strategic capital platform," noted the analysts.

On the industrial REITs as a sector, Raymond James said the leasing decisions that were being delayed due to election uncertainty are now being delayed due to policy uncertainty. The mark-to-market opportunity is still attractive though — now in the mid-30% range — and will drive mid single-digit NOI growth next year.

data.title: Vulcan Materials to acquire Superior Ready Mix Concrete, terms not disclosed
data.description:
• Vulcan Materials (NYSE:VMC) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Superior Ready Mix Concrete.
  • This value-enhancing acquisition will add six aggregates operations with more than 50 years of quality aggregates reserves to Vulcan's California franchise.
  • Superior will also enhance Vulcan's customer service capabilities in Southern California with two asphalt plants and thirteen ready-mixed concrete locations.
  • "With the financial strength and flexibility to continue to grow, we are well-positioned to continue to deliver value for our shareholders and our customers." said Tom Hill, Vulcan's Chairman and CEO.
  • The transaction is expected to close by the end of this year.

data.title: Fidelity Minerals to raise money via flow-through units
data.description:
• Fidelity Minerals (TSXV:FMN:CA) intends to undertake a non-brokered private placement offering of up to 1.67M flow-through units at a price of C$0.06 per FT Unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$100,200.
  • Each FT unit shall consist of one flow-through common share in the capital of the company and one non-flow-through warrant, with each warrant exercisable at a price of C$0.09 for a period of 24 months after the closing date of the financing.
  • The proceeds will be used by the company for eligible exploration expenditures.

data.title: Regions Financial appoints Roger W. Jenkins to serve on the board
data.description:
• Regions Financial (NYSE:RF) announced Roger W. Jenkins, director and CEO of Murphy Oil Corporation, has been appointed to serve on the board of the company and its subsidiary, Regions Bank.
  • Jenkins’ term on the Regions boards will begin January 1, 2025.
  • With the addition of Jenkins, the Regions boards will consist of 14 members.
  • Upon joining the Regions boards, Jenkins will serve on the Risk and Technology Committees.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple working with Broadcom to develop in-house AI server chips: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is working with Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) to develop the company's first in-house server chip exclusively designed for artificial intelligence, The Information reported.

The chip, known internally as Baltra, could be ready for mass production by 2026, the news outlet reported, citing people with direct knowledge of the matter. The new AI chip could be used for inferencing, which has the chip capable of processing new data and then hands them off to the large language models to produce an output, a task different than training models, the news outlet added.

The chip will be produced by Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and likely use its 3 nanometer process, The Information added, citing sources.

Broadcom shares rose 3.8% in early trading, while Apple shares rose 0.5%. Nvidia (NVDA), which dominates the AI accelerator market, rose 0.5%.

The plan to bring its own chips to its own servers was born three years ago, but the plan was accelerated after OpenAI's ChatGPT chatbot took the world by storm in December 2022, according to previous reports.

Chief Executive Tim Cook has said previously Apple has some advantages that its competitors in the AI space do not. "We believe in the transformative power and promise of AI, and we believe we have advantages that will differentiate us in this new era, including Apple's unique combination of seamless hardware, software and services integration, groundbreaking Apple's silicon, with our industry-leading neural engines and our unwavering focus on privacy, which underpins everything we create," Cook said on the company's earnings call in May.

Last week, Apple executive Benoit Dupin gave a rare endorsement of Amazon's (AMZN) custom artificial intelligence chips and said the iPhone maker is evaluating Amazon's latest AI chip for pre-training models.

Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple has also used some of Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) chips to train AI models for Apple Intelligence.

The news comes as Apple continued to roll out its Apple Intelligence features on Wednesday. The tech giant released the latest versions of iOS, macOS and iPadOS, all of which include several AI-related features, including the integration of ChatGPT into Siri.

Apple and Broadcom did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: E2open shares fall after Goldman downgrades it to SELL
data.description:
• Shares of E2open Parent Holdings (NYSE:ETWO) opened 8% lower on Wednesday, after Goldman Sachs downgraded it to SELL from NEUTRAL, citing limited opportunity for meaningful growth.
  • The investment firm also cut its price target for E2open Parent (ETWO) to $2.90 from $3.50.
  • Goldman believes ETWO shares are likely to remain under pressure as historically low retention levels, organic sales challenges, and a constrained balance sheet limit the opportunity for the business to return to meaningful growth over the next 12–18 months.
  • “With four consecutive quarters of yoy declines in subscription revenue growth and lowered FY25 guidance, we believe it is prudent to be incrementally cautious around shares of ETWO despite the company trading near trough levels,” Goldman analysts wrote in a research note.
  • Goldman said its updated target multiple reflects slower growth expectations and continued uncertainty around the pace of ETWO’s growth recovery. The target price implies ~7% downside.
